finding: Binary choices made before target determination show accuracy above chance when targets are emotionally arousing (erotic or negative), suggesting individuals access information about future stimulus locations. Effect magnitude increases with trait stimulus-seeking tendency.

Threat-related facial expressions dominate approachability judgments regardless of body cues; facial expressions exert stronger influence on composite ratings than body expressions alone, though angry body expressions suppress the effect of non-threatening facial expressions.

Chronic loneliness is associated with increased frequency, duration, and temperature preference for bathing. Physical coldness exposure temporarily increases reported loneliness; warm physical experiences reduce affiliation needs triggered by social rejection, suggesting substitutability between physical and social warmth.

Odd numbers are implicitly associated with masculinity and even numbers with femininity, automatically biasing gender judgments of arbitrarily paired ambiguous stimuli (names, faces) despite participants' lack of conscious awareness of the numerical influence.

Processing word sequences that progress across conceptual domains produces higher positive affect than processing sequences confined to a single concept, independent of variability or plurality of content.

Hierarchical social structures are processed more fluently (faster identification, quicker learning, easier decision-making) than non-hierarchical structures, and this processing ease increases preference for and attribution of positive qualities to hierarchies.
